---
title: "2b Activity: Devcontainers and Codespaces"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    css: _materials.css
    include-after-body: _progress.html
---


## Goal

We will add our own devcontainer configuration to the practice repository from `2a`.

Note: The checkboxes on this page are only stored locally in your browser. They are not submitted or synced anywhere.

::: {.guide-progress}

Progress: <span data-progress-count>0</span> / <span data-progress-total>0</span> steps complete.

<button class="reset-progress" type="button" data-progress-reset>Reset progress</button>

:::


## Activity

::: {.panel-tabset}

## 1. Open The Practice Repo

Start on GitHub in Chrome or Edge.

1. Open the `python_tmr_practice` repository you created in `2a`.
1. Click the green **Code** button.
1. Click the **Codespaces** tab.
1. Open the existing Codespace, or create a new one if needed.

::: {.task-list}

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="2b-open-practice-repo">
  <span>I opened the practice repository in Codespaces.</span>
</label>

:::


## 2. Add Devcontainer Files

In VS Code Web:

1. Open the Command Palette with **F1** or **Shift+Command+P**.
1. Search for **add dev**.
1. Choose **Codespaces: Add Dev Container Configuration Files...**.
1. Choose a Python configuration.
1. If asked for a Python version, choose Python 3.13 if it is available. Otherwise, accept the default.
1. Accept the remaining default options.

The exact list of choices can change a little over time. The important thing is that we are adding a `.devcontainer/` folder to the repository.

::: {.task-list}

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="2b-add-devcontainer">
  <span>I added devcontainer configuration files from the Command Palette.</span>
</label>

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="2b-see-folder">
  <span>I found the `.devcontainer/` folder in the Explorer.</span>
</label>

:::


## 3. Inspect the Recipe

Open `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`.

Look for:

- the name of the configuration;
- the base image or feature choices;
- any VS Code extension recommendations;
- any setup commands.

Do not worry if your file does not match the course repository.

::: {.task-list}

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="2b-open-devcontainer-json">
  <span>I opened `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`.</span>
</label>

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="2b-identify-recipe-piece">
  <span>I identified at least one thing this file controls.</span>
</label>

:::


## 4. Rebuild if Prompted

VS Code may ask whether you want to rebuild or reopen the Codespace using the new configuration.

Once you're finished editing, you can follow the prompts to rebuild or use the Command Palette to search for and choose **Codespaces: Rebuild Container**.

The key thing to note is that the repository now contains our environment recipe.

::: {.task-list}

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="2b-rebuild-decision">
  <span>I can explain why changing the devcontainer may require rebuilding the Codespace.</span>
</label>

:::


## 5. Commit And Push

Use Source Control:

1. Review the `.devcontainer/` changes.
1. Find the `.devcontainer/` files under **Changes**.
1. Hover over each file and click the **+** button to stage it.
1. Confirm that the files moved from **Changes** to **Staged Changes**.
1. Commit with a message such as:

```text
Added Python devcontainer configuration.
```

Push or sync the commit to GitHub.

::: {.task-list}

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="2b-review-devcontainer-diff">
  <span>I reviewed the `.devcontainer/` changes.</span>
</label>

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="2b-commit-devcontainer">
  <span>I committed the `.devcontainer/` configuration.</span>
</label>

<label class="task-check">
  <input type="checkbox" data-progress-id="2b-push-devcontainer">
  <span>I pushed or synced the commit to GitHub.</span>
</label>

:::

:::
